hypothesis: If pre-trained single-gas neural networks are used as feature extractors within a graph neural network architecture to encode gas-specific plasma behavior, then the resulting model will more accurately predict mixture etching rates under extrapolative conditions (unseen process parameters and gas compositions) compared to a standard feedforward neural network trained directly on mixture data, because the graph structure explicitly captures pairwise gas interactions while the frozen single-gas networks provide physically meaningful representations that generalize beyond the training distribution.
